name: Valdemadera and Alhama Greenway Loop
name_original: Valdemadera – circular por Inestrillas y Cervera del Río Alhama
description: This difficult 21.1-mile touring bicycle route near Valdemadera offers historical sites like Contrebia Leucade and scenic river valleys.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Aguilar del Río Alhama traverse a low-altitude mountainous area within the foothills of the Iberian System. The region is characterized by wooded slopes, river valleys, and striking rocky crags, shaped by the Alhama river. Cyclists can explore routes along the river, through vegetable gardens, and past expansive holm oak mountains like Monegro. This diverse terrain offers varied experiences for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Aguilar Del Río Alhama The most popular touring cycling route is Valdemadera and Alhama Greenway Loop, a 21.1 miles (33.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 53 minutes to complete. This route follows the picturesque Alhama Greenway along the river. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is El Val Reservoir – Ágreda Historic Center loop from Estación de Ágreda, a difficult 24.4 miles (39.3 km) path. This route offers views of El Val Reservoir and passes through the historic center of Ágreda. Local touring cyclists also love the Cervera del Río Alhama – Church of Castilruiz loop from Cervera del Río Alhama, a 47.0 miles (75.7 km) trail leading through river valleys and past historical sites, often completed in about 5 hours 26 minutes. Touring cycling around Aguilar Del Río Alhama is defined by river valleys, wooded slopes, and rocky crags. The network offers options for different ability levels, though many routes present a challenge. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 40 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Aguilar Del Río Alhama's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Aguilar del Río Alhama?
A: There are over 10 touring cycling routes around Aguilar del Río Alhama documented on komoot, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists. Most of these routes are considered difficult, with one moderate option.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Aguilar del Río Alhama?
A: The region features a low-altitude mountainous area within the foothills of the Iberian System. You'll encounter diverse terrain including wooded slopes, river valleys shaped by the Alhama river, and striking rocky crags. Routes can also lead through vegetable gardens and past expansive holm oak mountains.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Aguilar del Río Alhama?
A: While many routes in Aguilar del Río Alhama are classified as difficult, there is one moderate touring cycling route available. Beginners should be prepared for varied terrain and elevation changes typical of a low-altitude mountainous area. For a less strenuous option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994475780/valdemadera-and-alhama-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdemadera and Alhama Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the picturesque Alhama Greenway.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, the majority of touring cycling routes in Aguilar del Río Alhama are classified as difficult, providing a significant challenge for experienced cyclists. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31816062" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cervera del Río Alhama – Church of Castilruiz loop from Cervera del Río Alhama&lt;/a&gt;, spanning over 75 km with substantial elevation gain, are ideal for those seeking a demanding ride.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes in Aguilar del Río Alhama are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994475780/valdemadera-and-alhama-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdemadera and Alhama Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31814028" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Val Reservoir – Ágreda Historic Center loop from Estación de Ágreda&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or viewpoints can I see while cycling?
A: The region offers stunning natural beauty. You can cycle along the Alhama river, explore the Sierra de Alcarama, and ascend Monegro, an expansive holm oak mountain with panoramic views. Notable highlights include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5573920" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Tío Fresquito&lt;/a&gt; for views, the unique rock formations of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3598457" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Roscas&lt;/a&gt;, and the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5575390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Val Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The Sierra del Tormo is also home to a colony of rupicolous birds of prey.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural attractions accessible by touring bike?
A: Absolutely. The area is rich in history. You can visit the significant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5338604" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Celtiberian Archaeological Site of Contrebia Leucade&lt;/a&gt;, explore the 16th-century Iglesia de la Asunción, or discover the 12th-century Romanesque Ermita de Santa María la Antigua. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31814028" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Val Reservoir – Ágreda Historic Center loop from Estación de Ágreda&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, passes through the historic center of Ágreda.

Q: Can I find routes that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, for those interested in natural water features, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de las Truchas Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a highlight in the region, offering a picturesque stop during your cycling adventure.

Q: Is it possible to combine cycling with a visit to a spa?
A: Yes, the Balneario de la Albotea, a nearby spa, offers an opportunity for relaxation and wellness, making it a potential stop or destination to combine with your touring cycling itinerary in the region.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Aguilar del Río Alhama?
A: Given its low-altitude mountainous setting, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for touring cycling in Aguilar del Río Alhama, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be warm, while winter might bring colder weather, though the region's lower altitude often avoids extreme conditions.

Q: Are there any routes that specifically follow the Alhama river?
A: Yes, the Alhama Greenway is a dedicated route that offers a picturesque journey along the right bank of the Alhama river. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994475780/valdemadera-and-alhama-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdemadera and Alhama Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option that incorporates this scenic path.

Q: What are some of the longer touring cycling routes available?
A: For longer rides, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31816062" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cervera del Río Alhama – Church of Castilruiz loop from Cervera del Río Alhama&lt;/a&gt; covers approximately 75.7 km (47.0 miles), typically taking over 5 hours to complete. Another substantial option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31815569" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cervera del Río Alhama – Yerga Summit loop from Cervera del Río Alhama&lt;/a&gt;, at around 54.3 km (33.7 miles).
tips:
Remains of the oldest population settlements in La Rioja, located on the right bank of the Alhama river.

The pelendones, a Celtiberian people, settled in Contrebia Leukade in the Iron Age and built a fortification, urbanizing the land and building their houses carved into the rock.

Later, during the second half of the 4th century B.C. Different tribes arrived that expanded the town and reinforced it with a deep ditch dug into the rock and a wall that surrounded the entire city.

This spectacular archaeological site can be toured with a guided tour. It is mandatory to book by phone (941 19 71 19) or by email (contrebialeucade@gmail.com).

At the foot of the Sierra de Alcarama is the picturesque town of Cervera del Río Alhama.

Due to its excellent location, the town was hotly contested between Arabs and Christians. At the beginning of the 7th century the town was invaded by Muslims, leaving a great architectural heritage.

The town is also known for being one of the main producers of espadrilles, a shoe originating in ancient Egypt. In the same municipality is the Alpargata Museum, where the evolution of the sector is shown, from artisan manufacturing to its industrialization.

Ruins of a large defensive fortress of Muslim origin on the rock of San Antonio, built between the 11th and 12th centuries.

The set consists of a central core formed by the keep, an esplanade protected by a wall and a second outer defensive line.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Valdemadera circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this route is quite varied. While some sections, particularly parts of the Camino Verde del Alhama, offer a firm surface suitable for cycling, other segments involve narrow paths, dirt tracks, and potentially steep inclines. You can expect a mix of surfaces, traversing through fertile river valleys, dramatic rocky outcrops, and Mediterranean scrubland.

faqs.question: What type of bicycle is best suited for this route?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain, which includes dirt tracks and potentially unpaved sections, this route is generally more suitable for hybrid or gravel bikes rather than traditional road touring bikes. A bike with good suspension and wider tires will provide a more comfortable and capable ride.

faqs.question: What historical and natural sights can I see along the Valdemadera circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is rich in history and natural beauty. You'll pass by the significant Celtiberian Archaeological Site of Contrebia Leucade, and charming towns like Cervera del Río Alhama, which boasts a rich history and the impressive Cervera Castle. The landscape features limestone cliffs, the fertile Alhama river valley, and diverse flora and fauna, including raptors.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Valdemadera circular route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for the entire circular route aren't detailed, you can typically find parking within the towns along the route, such as Inestrillas or Cervera del Río Alhama. These villages serve as good access points for starting your ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region experiences a Mediterranean climate. Spring and autumn are generally the best times to cycle, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summers can be hot, and winters might bring colder conditions, though the Mediterranean scrubland remains scenic year-round.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this touring bicycle trail?
faqs.answer: There is no specific information prohibiting dogs on this route. However, it's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through villages, near archaeological sites, or in areas with wildlife, to ensure their safety and respect for the environment and local regulations.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to cycle this route. It traverses public lands and existing paths. However, if you plan to visit any specific archaeological sites or attractions, they might have their own entry fees.

faqs.question: Does the Valdemadera circular route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of other well-known paths. Notably, it includes significant portions of the Camino Verde del Alhama, and may also intersect with parts of the Sierras Riojanas and Camino de Comago a Valdemadera.

faqs.question: Are there any notable natural features or viewpoints on the trail?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. The trail offers picturesque views of the Alhama river valley, dramatic rocky outcrops that are home to raptors, and Mediterranean scrubland with aromatic plants. On clear days, you might even catch a glimpse of the Moncayo Mountain in the distance. The area is also part of the Natura 2000 network, recognized for its special bird protection areas.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the towns along the route, such as Cervera del Río Alhama and Inestrillas, offer amenities. You can find cafes and restaurants in Cervera del Río Alhama, and both towns may have options for accommodation, including hotels or rural houses, for those looking to extend their stay.
